steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The student claims transgenic fish in the wild may have negative ecological effects. The decisive clue is "significant negative long-term ecological effects", which demands a quote showing how the fish could cause harm.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice D explains that interbreeding could spread fluorescence, making wild fish more vulnerable to predators—a clear ecological consequence of genetic modification. A only notes sex ratios without impact, B describes a lack of data, and C questions the completeness of observations. That matches choice D — D is correct.
